---
title: "Sample RMarkdown Document"
author: "Test Author"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: html_document
params:
  year: 2026
---

## Introduction

This is a sample RMarkdown document for testing the Obsidian plugin.
It contains various R code chunk types and inline R expressions.

## Basic R Code Chunk

```{r}
x <- 1:10
mean(x)
```

## Named Chunk with Options

```{r summary-stats, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>%
  group_by(cyl) %>%
  summarise(avg_mpg = mean(mpg))
```

## Plot Chunk

```{r my-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, fig.cap="A scatter plot"}
plot(mtcars$wt, mtcars$mpg,
     xlab = "Weight", ylab = "MPG",
     main = "Weight vs MPG")
```

## Hidden Chunk (echo=FALSE)

```{r, echo=FALSE}
secret_value <- 42
```

## Python Engine

```{python}
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3]})
print(df)
```

## SQL Engine

```{sql, connection=con, output.var="result"}
SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE year = 2026
```

## Inline R Code

The dataset has `r nrow(mtcars)` observations and `r ncol(mtcars)` variables.
The mean MPG is `r round(mean(mtcars$mpg), 2)`.

## Standard Markdown Features

These should render normally:

- **Bold text** and *italic text*
- [A link](https://example.com)
- An image: ![alt text](image.png)

### Code block (non-R)

```javascript
console.log("This is not R code");
```

> A blockquote should render normally too.

| Column A | Column B |
|----------|----------|
| 1        | 2        |
| 3        | 4        |
